commentary: Saturday long run with the Lakefront Pacers — 12 miles at HM pace, three weeks out from race day. The 2 AM trial-prep brain is real but the 7 AM legs are realer. Off-the-clock for once.

Volunteered at the CARPLS Saturday legal-aid intake clinic this morning. Three hours, six clients, four resolved on the spot. The reminder I needed: representation closes a lot of gaps faster than people think.
Reposted a fantastic piece from @bryan_stevenson on indigent defense funding gaps. The data is hard. The work is harder. Recommended reading for anyone in the system.
Heading into trial prep on two complex felony cases starting October. The discovery box situation in my office could be its own felony. Grateful for paralegals.

Honored to serve on the Chicago Bar Association's annual CLE committee for the 2026 program. Strong panel forming on indigent defense funding for the Nov 12 program — registration open at chicagobar.org.
Tuesday speed work at the lakefront track. 8x400 at goal pace, last one fastest. Three weeks until Nov 1. The training plan is the only argument I'm winning this month.

Excited to congratulate Lorraine Okafor on her retirement announcement at the office this week — twenty-three years of supervising attorneys who didn't deserve her, and she still made us better. Public defense in this county owes her a debt that won't show up in a single article.
Reading recommendation: Just Mercy by Bryan Stevenson, again. Some books you keep on the desk, not the shelf.
Knee cleared by Dr. Brian Leong at Northside Athletic Medicine — strength block stays, race day stays Nov 1. Patellofemoral pain respects no one's schedule, including a half-marathon taper.
Four years next September at the Cook County Public Defender's Office. The cases stay heavy. The people stay good. Some weeks both feel impossible at the same time.
Loyola Law alumni event tonight. Saw a few familiar faces from the 2L Trial Advocacy section. They are also tired and that helps.
Saturday brunch crew at Cozy Corner after a 12-mile long run. The veggie omelet is the highest-value food per dollar in Wicker Park, fight me.

Marcus Traylor and I were sworn in the same week in 2022. Today he covered three of my courtrooms while I prepped for trial #1. Friends like that are how the docket survives. Will return the favor in October when his trial hits.
Naomi at mile 8 with a sign that says 'I drove him to law school, the least he can do is run 13.1 miles.' Sisters are unbeatable hype crews.
Public defenders we need: more Spanish-language interpreters, more mitigation specialists, more sleep. The first two are recruitable. The third is a personal problem.
Filed a 12(b) motion at 1:48 AM and the only witness was the lukewarm chamomile. Now to bed. Sleep is also evidence.
